title: Major AI Copyright Settlement Approved for Publishers and Authors
subtitle: A US judge has finalized a massive agreement involving the AI company Anthropic and thousands of writers.

summary.en: A US judge recently approved a $1.5 billion copyright settlement between Anthropic and thousands of authors. This agreement follows a legal battle over the use of books to train AI chatbots. Bloomsbury, the publisher of Harry Potter, is among those receiving a significant payment. This case is considered the largest copyright recovery in history. It marks a turning point in how AI companies handle the work of creative professionals.
summary.zh: 美國法官近期批准了 Anthropic 與數千名作者之間十五億美元的著作權和解協議。這項協議結束了關於使用書籍訓練人工智慧聊天機器人的法律爭議。出版《哈利波特》的布魯姆斯伯里出版公司是獲得大額賠償的對象之一。此案被視為史上金額最高的著作權賠償，標誌著人工智慧公司處理專業創作者作品方式的轉捩點。

paragraphs.en: A significant legal chapter in the world of artificial intelligence has come to a close. According to The Guardian Technology, a US district judge has officially approved a $1.5 billion settlement between the AI company Anthropic and thousands of authors. This decision follows a long-running legal dispute regarding how AI systems, such as the Claude chatbot, are built. The core of the issue was that these systems were trained on vast amounts of data from the internet, which included books and articles protected by copyright—the legal right that gives creators control over how their work is used.
paragraphs.zh: 人工智慧領域的一項重大法律篇章已告一段落。根據《衛報》科技版報導，一位美國地方法官已正式批准人工智慧公司 Anthropic 與數千名作者之間達成的十五億美元和解協議。這項裁決源於一場關於 Claude 聊天機器人等人工智慧系統如何建構的長期法律糾紛。問題的核心在於，這些系統是利用網路上海量的資料進行訓練，其中包含了受著作權保護的書籍與文章，而著作權即是賦予創作者對其作品使用方式擁有控制權的法律權利。

paragraphs.en: Bloomsbury, the famous publisher of the Harry Potter series, is one of the major groups set to benefit from this decision. The company reported that over 14,000 of its titles were included in the settlement. According to The Guardian Technology, Bloomsbury expects to receive a total of about $19 million after paying for legal fees. The company plans to share this money with the authors whose work was used. This payout is part of a larger plan where authors receive compensation for their intellectual property.
paragraphs.zh: 出版《哈利波特》系列的知名出版商布魯姆斯伯里（Bloomsbury）是此次裁決的主要受益集團之一。該公司表示，旗下超過一萬四千部作品被納入此次和解範圍。根據《衛報》科技版報導，布魯姆斯伯里預計在支付法律費用後，將獲得總計約一千九百萬美元的賠償。該公司計畫將這筆款項與作品被使用過的作者分享。這筆賠償金是作者因其智慧財產權獲得補償的大型計畫之一。

paragraphs.en: This case began in 2024 when novelist Andrea Bartz and two other writers filed a lawsuit. They argued that AI companies should ask for permission before using creative work to train their programs. In contrast, many US-based AI companies have defended their actions by using the legal idea of 'fair use.' This rule allows people to use copyrighted material without permission in certain specific situations. However, many artists and writers around the world have disagreed, arguing that they should be paid for their contributions to AI development.
paragraphs.zh: 此案始於二零二四年，當時小說家安德莉亞·巴茨（Andrea Bartz）與另外兩名作家提起訴訟。他們主張人工智慧公司在利用創意作品訓練程式前，應先取得許可。相對地，許多美國的人工智慧公司則以「合理使用」的法律概念為其行為辯護。這項規則允許人們在特定情況下，無需許可即可使用受著作權保護的素材。然而，全球許多藝術家與作家對此表示反對，認為他們對人工智慧發展的貢獻應獲得報酬。

paragraphs.en: Judge Araceli Martínez-Olguín, who oversaw the case, stated that the settlement provides 'meaningful relief' to the authors and publishers involved. Justin Nelson, the lead lawyer for the authors, called the result 'the largest known copyright recovery in history.' With about 91% of the 482,000 works covered in the lawsuit already claimed, it is clear that many creators are ready to move forward. Aparna Sridhar, a deputy general counsel at Anthropic, expressed satisfaction that the matter is coming to a close.
paragraphs.zh: 負責審理此案的法官阿拉塞利·馬丁內斯-奧爾金（Araceli Martínez-Olguín）表示，這項和解協議為相關作者與出版商提供了「實質性的救濟」。代表作者方的首席律師賈斯汀·尼爾森（Justin Nelson）稱此結果為「史上已知金額最高的著作權賠償」。在訴訟涵蓋的四十八萬兩千部作品中，已有約百分之九十一的作品完成索賠，顯見許多創作者已準備好向前邁進。Anthropic 的副總法律顧問阿帕娜·斯里達爾（Aparna Sridhar）則對此事件即將落幕表示滿意。

paragraphs.en: This settlement is the first major outcome among dozens of similar lawsuits currently moving through the US court system. It highlights the growing tension between the rapid growth of technology and the rights of those who create content. As AI continues to change how we work and learn, the question of how to fairly pay creators remains a central topic of debate.
paragraphs.zh: 這項和解是目前美國法院系統中數十起類似訴訟案裡，首個取得重大進展的案例。它凸顯了科技的快速成長與內容創作者權益之間日益緊張的關係。隨著人工智慧持續改變我們工作與學習的方式，如何公平地支付創作者報酬，仍是辯論的核心議題。

paragraphs.en: Looking ahead, the industry is already changing how it handles these relationships. Bloomsbury has already started an AI licensing deal. This allows the publisher to sell academic works to be used for training AI programs in a legal way. Under this system, authors can choose to 'opt in'—meaning they give their permission—and will receive royalties, which are regular payments made to creators for the use of their work. As more companies consider similar schemes, the relationship between AI developers and the creative community may become more structured and balanced. While this settlement marks a major milestone, it is likely just the beginning of a much longer conversation about fairness in the age of artificial intelligence.
paragraphs.zh: 展望未來，產業已開始改變處理這些關係的方式。布魯姆斯伯里已啟動一項人工智慧授權協議，允許該出版商以合法方式販售學術著作，供人工智慧程式訓練使用。在此制度下，作者可以選擇「加入」，意即給予許可，並獲得版稅，也就是創作者因作品被使用而定期收到的報酬。隨著更多公司考慮類似方案，人工智慧開發者與創意社群之間的關係可能會變得更有條理且更平衡。雖然這項和解標誌著一個重要的里程碑，但這很可能只是人工智慧時代關於公平性議題，一場更漫長對話的開端。

keyTerms.definitionEn: A distinct period or phase in a series of events.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見指書的「章節」，這裡比喻法律訴訟的一個階段。文中：A significant legal chapter in the world of artificial intelligence has come to a close.

keyTerms.definitionEn: An official agreement intended to resolve a dispute or conflict.
keyTerms.noteZh: 常見指「定居」或「殖民」，在法律語境下指雙方達成和解。文中：According to The Guardian Technology, a US district judge has officially approved a $1.5 billion settlement between the AI company Anthropic and thousands of authors.

keyTerms.definitionEn: To choose to participate in something or give permission for something.
keyTerms.noteZh: 由動詞 opt 和介詞 in 組成，意思並非字面上的「選擇進入」，而是指主動同意參與某項計畫。文中：Under this system, authors can choose to 'opt in'—meaning they give their permission—and will receive royalties, which are regular payments made to creators for the use of their work.

sources.name: The Guardian Technology
sources.title: Harry Potter publisher to receive millions in Anthropic copyright settlement

previousArticles.title: Major AI Copyright Settlement Approved by US Judge
previousArticles.summaryEn: A US judge has approved a $1.5 billion settlement between the AI company Anthropic and thousands of authors. The case focused on the use of copyrighted books and articles to train AI chatbots without permission. Bloomsbury, the publisher of Harry Potter, is among the companies receiving a large payment. This is the largest copyright recovery in history and marks a significant moment for the AI industry. The settlement highlights the ongoing tension between technology companies and creative professionals over intellectual property.

quiz.question: What is the specific financial outcome for Bloomsbury following the settlement?

options.A: They will receive $1.5 billion in total compensation.
options.B: They expect to keep approximately $19 million after legal costs.
options.C: They will pay $19 million to the authors of the Harry Potter series.
options.D: They will receive a share of the settlement based on 482,000 works.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。根據文章第二段，Bloomsbury 預計在支付法律費用後，將獲得約 1900 萬美元的賠償，並計劃與作者分享這筆錢。選項 A 的 15 億美元是總和解金額，而非 Bloomsbury 個別獲得的金額。選項 C 錯誤，因為 1900 萬是他們收到的錢，而非支付給作者的賠償總額。選項 D 雖然提到了 482,000 部作品，但那是整個訴訟涵蓋的範圍，而非 Bloomsbury 的賠償計算基準。

quiz.question: What can be inferred about the future of AI training in the publishing industry?

options.A: Publishers will likely stop allowing AI companies to use their books entirely.
options.B: Authors will no longer have control over how their work is used by AI.
options.C: The industry is shifting toward a model where authors are compensated through licensing agreements.
options.D: AI companies will stop using copyrighted material to avoid future lawsuits.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 C。根據文章最後一段，Bloomsbury 已開始實施 AI 授權交易，讓作者可以選擇加入（opt in）並獲得版稅。這顯示出版業正從法律訴訟轉向結構化的授權模式。選項 A 與文章提到的「授權協議」相矛盾。選項 B 錯誤，因為文章明確提到作者可以選擇是否參與（opt in）。選項 D 過於武斷，文章僅提到產業正在尋求更平衡的合作關係，並未表示 AI 公司會完全停止使用受版權保護的材料。

quiz.question: In the final paragraph, what does the word 'royalties' refer to?

options.A: A one-time payment made to purchase the copyright of a book.
options.B: Regular payments made to creators for the ongoing use of their work.
options.C: Legal fees paid to lawyers during a copyright settlement.
options.D: A tax imposed on AI companies for using internet data.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 B。文章最後一段明確定義 royalties 為「regular payments made to creators for the use of their work」（為使用創作者作品而支付的定期報酬）。選項 A 錯誤，因為版稅通常是基於使用量而非一次性買斷版權。選項 C 的法律費用在第二段已由「legal fees」一詞表達，並非版稅。選項 D 與稅務無關，這是商業授權行為。

quiz.question: What is the primary message of this article?

options.A: The legal battle between Andrea Bartz and Anthropic has resulted in the bankruptcy of AI companies.
options.B: Bloomsbury is the only publisher successfully challenging AI companies in court.
options.C: The $1.5 billion settlement is the final and only solution to AI copyright issues.
options.D: A major settlement marks a turning point in the ongoing debate over fair compensation for creators in the AI era.

quiz.explanationZh: 正確答案為 D。本文主旨探討 AI 發展與創作者權利之間的緊張關係，並以這項和解案作為一個里程碑，說明產業正朝向更結構化的授權模式發展。選項 A 的破產說法文章未提及。選項 B 錯誤，因為文章提到這是「數十起」訴訟中的其中一起。選項 C 錯誤，因為文章最後一段提到這只是「更長對話的開始」，而非終局。
